calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Shannon Kidd and Jeffory Kidd's unopposed Motion for Summary Judgment or in the Alternative Summary Adjudication as to First and Second Causes of Action is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

(see word minutes for full details)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission To Plaintiff Laura Kerimova, Set One Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SHANNON KIDD'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission To Plaintiff Laura Kerimova, Set One Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant Shannon Kidd's unopposed Motion To Deem Requests For Admission To Plaintiff Laura Kerimova, Set One, is GRANTED.

Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280(c), the court shall deem unanswered requests for admissions (RFAs) admitted, unless it finds the responding party, before hearing on the motion, served a proposed response to the requests for admission that is in substantial compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.220.

The record here demonstrates Defendant Shannon Kidd sent requests for admissions that appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) Plaintiff Laura Kerimova failed to answer/respond. The record does not demonstrate her failure was reasonable or justified. The requests set out in Defendant's Request for Admissions, Set One, are deemed admitted.

The record does not show Plaintiff acted with substantial justification in connection with these RFAs. Plaintiff Laura Kerimova shall pay $860 to Defendant Shannon Kidd in sanctions by October 15, 2025.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Stephanie Taft, Esq., of QUILL & ARROW, LLP. Tel:(310)933-4271 for plaintiff.
Lisa Giarratno, Esq., of Deputy City Attorney. Tel: (415) 554-3813 for defendant City and County of San Francisco.
Court Repoter: Brianna Rudd CSR# 13668 email: BrainnaRudd@gmail.com Tel: 209.747.1306.
No other appearances made in this matter.

A hearing is required. The matter was heard and discussed. Upon conclusion of the proceedings rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF LAURA KERIMOVA's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is granted.

Order is Signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Court Repoter: Brianna Rudd CSR# 13668 email: BriannaRudd@gmail.com Tel: 209.747.1306. Reported. (302/HEK)
